summary: I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) People & Talent professional who thrives on solving problems through data analysis, building processes grounded in evidence, and collaborating with stakeholders for sustainable impact. Expect me to always be seeking ways we can run a tighter, more human talent operation—through organized, detail-driven coordination.

bullets.text: Manage high-complexity {coord|coordination, logistics, and scheduling} for 20+ programs in partnership with cross-functional teams and remote staff
Establish {proc|processes and documentation} for 70+ staff, demonstrating a process-improvement mindset that reduced onboarding time by 75%
Serve as primary point of {comm|contact} for stakeholders, using polished written and verbal communication to coordinate across teams
Facilitate listening sessions with 25 global {cross|stakeholders} to align on logistics, requirements, and contingencies
Managed {rec|recruiting} logistics by drafting job descriptions, posting openings, and coordinating a challenging international search
Engaged candidates via personalized {comm|communication} and strong candidate experience, increasing applications by 566% in 2 months
Organized {coord|recruiting events} and moderated panels with C-suite at top 3 universities, reaching 230+ students and 80+ applicants
Developed proficiency across HR functions, from {rec|recruitment} and compensation to performance management
Analyzed 2,000+ employee experiences to guide C-suite decisions on satisfaction drivers
Led a manager effectiveness initiative, transforming feedback data into actionable reports
Provided consultations to improve strategic plans and internal training across multiple functions
Improved employee experience via 5 {cross|cross-functional} projects spanning survey development, rewards & recognition, and L&D
Delivered HR support for business priorities across {rec|recruiting}, headcount, and succession planning
Optimized onboarding processes for new hires across 3 global locations through {proc|process analysis}
Supported the HRBP team by piloting an Inclusive Leadership workshop, later scaled to 23 global locations
Strengthened {cross|engagement} via an HR newsletter and award-winning well-being initiatives, recognized among 1,000+ competitors
As a team of consultants, partnered with an HR client to audit 300+ leave cases, quantifying $362K to refute assumptions
Empowered the HR team to present executive-ready data insights, driving policy redesigns and first aid training

changeLog: 2026-05-31: Tailored for Samsara — Recruiting Coordinator (SF). Broad umbrella 'People & Talent.' Location → San Francisco, CA (role's office). Strong fit. Led InGenius with high-complexity coordination/logistics/scheduling, process-improvement mindset, primary point-of-contact + polished communication (JD's exact language). Kronos surfaces recruiting logistics + candidate communication. Skills → ATS (Greenhouse, Lever) + Workday (JD-named: Greenhouse, Lever, Workday, Slack, Google Calendar). ODN collective. Counts fixed (InGenius/Kronos/NextGen/Vestas=4, ODN=2). Note: JD also values executive travel/calendar coordination — Jamie's coordination is program/stakeholder logistics, framed truthfully without claiming exec-travel.
